text.content:
Triangulating the Stair

Phase 7's investigative spine is a lightweight system. The party collects evidence in five categories. Different combinations produce different outcomes.

The five categories

#CategoryConfirms
1Name“Seventh Stair” or VII Stair is the route's working name.
2FunctionThe route is a descent, not a metaphor, port code, or surface road.
3PermissionThe route is controlled by authorization (a permission to descend), not just geography. One is admitted; one does not simply find it.
4MaterialWax, stone dust, mineral residue, seed ash, ink, or route-marks tie the evidence to a specific region.
5PlaceOld mining records, survey maps, claim ledgers, or waymarks connect to a specific surface site — the Seven-Cut Cairn.

Triangulation outcomes

Categories collectedResult
0–1Party follows a false lead first (see Node 6 — The False Stair).
2Party reaches the right region (southern Cairn Hills) but not the site. Field search required.
3Party reaches the right region and can search effectively. Outcome C.
4Party locates the Seven-Cut Cairn. Outcome B (with some enemy preparation).
5Party locates it before enemy route-cleansers finish their work. Outcome A.

System flexibility

Triangulation does not require an exact sequence. The party may consult sources in any order. A single source may contribute to multiple categories. Captured NPCs are valid sources; so are letters, witness statements, and field observations.

Source-to-category guide

SourceCategories it can deliver
Iren Velash (Node 1)Name, Function, partial Place
Aldea Veren (Node 2)Material, Function, partial Place
Edrik Vone (Node 3)Permission, Place, Name
Guild + Dalmor Vetch (Node 4)Place, Function, Material
Captured Haskur (Node 5)Name, Permission
Captured Kethren (Node 5)Function, Permission, partial Place
Captured Korre (Node 5)Place, Permission, Name
Kestrel & Reed staff (Node 5)Permission, partial Place
The False Stair (Node 6)Reverse-evidence; the party learns one wrong thing they can rule out
Field reconnaissance (Node 7)Material, Place
Caelith by courierBackground on missing Yeomanry surveyors; can supply partial Place if asked correctly

Tracking the count

The GM should track categories without making the players see the tracking. Use the BR2 — Campaign State Snapshot macro to log them privately, or a simple sheet on your side of the screen. The players experience this as gradually understanding what they're looking at, not as filling in a progress bar.

Reward triangulation

If a single source could deliver everything, the phase would be a one-NPC errand. Cross-referencing two or three sources produces a sharper reading than the sum of its parts. Iren in particular reads better when she has more to read. Aldea reads better when given material that has been read first by Iren. Vone reads better when shown a name he has already filed under another spelling.

Anti-bottleneck rule

If the party gets stuck at 2 categories and is genuinely confused, give them a passive prompt: a courier delivers a re-copy of a previous handout with one new detail highlighted, or a friendly NPC remembers a phrase they overheard, or the False Stair appears as a planted misdirection that itself reveals what is being hidden. The phase must not dead-end.
